skill.name: flux-kontext
skill.description: Edit images with Flux 1 Kontext Pro (Black Forest Labs' precise local image-edit model) on RunComfy — bundled with the model's documented prompting patterns so the skill gets sharper output than naive prompting against the same model.
skill.summary: Edit images with Flux 1 Kontext Pro via the RunComfy CLI
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 53 patterns (36 external_commands, 9 network, 4 filesystem, 4 high-severity blockers). After semantic evaluation, ALL findings are FALSE POSITIVES. External commands are documentation examples in code blocks, not runtime code. Network URLs are legitimate RunComfy API endpoints. Filesystem references are example placeholders or standard config paths. High-severity 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flags are false positives from the word 'strong' in legitimate context. No malicious patterns detected.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Flag
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 4, 109, and 144. After semantic review, these are FALSE POSITIVES. The analyzer detected the word 'strong' in legitimate context (model strength, brand strength) and incorrectly classified it. No cryptographic algorithms are used in this file.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Lines 4, 109, and 144 contain no cryptographic operations. The word 'strong' appears in context of model capability ('Strong preservation under explicit instruction') and has no relation to cryptography.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Path Traversal Sequences
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged path traversal at lines 70 and 81. These are documentation placeholders (<absolute/path>) showing users where to specify their output directory. Not actual traversal vulnerabilities.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: <absolute/path> is a common documentation convention for output directory specification. The skill passes this directly to the CLI --output-dir parameter which is intentional behavior.

high_findings.title: False Positive: Hidden File Access
high_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'hidden file in home directory' and 'hidden file access' at line 163. This is the RunComfy token config file path (~/.config/runcomfy/token.json). This is legitimate configuration storage, not malicious hidden file access.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json is the standard RunComfy CLI configuration file. The skill documents where tokens are stored for security transparency, not for malicious access.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: External Command Documentation
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 36 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' as external_commands. These are all in markdown code blocks (```bash and inline `code`) showing CLI command examples. They are documentation, not executable code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All backtick patterns appear inside markdown code fences (```bash ... ```). These are literal command examples for users to copy, not Ruby execution contexts.

medium_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
medium_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'system reconnaissance' at line 41. This is model routing logic ('If the user said Flux Kontext explicitly, route here regardless'). This is legitimate skill dispatch, not reconnaissance.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 41 contains 'If the user said Flux Kontext / kontext / BFL Kontext explicitly, route here regardless.' This is standard skill routing based on user intent, not system enumeration.

low_findings.title: Informational: Network Endpoints to RunComfy API
low_findings.description: The skill references RunComfy service endpoints (runcomfy.com, model-api.runcomfy.net). These are legitimate third-party API endpoints required for the skill to function. No suspicious external URLs detected.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All network URLs point to the legitimate RunComfy service (model-api.runcomfy.net) which is the required backend for this image editing skill. No exfiltration endpoints detected.

content.user_title: Edit Images with Flux Kontext Pro on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Need precise local edits to images without regenerating the whole scene? Flux Kontext Pro delivers single-reference image editing with high fidelity and strong prompt control, accessible via simple RunComfy CLI commands.
actual_capabilities: Single-reference precise local edits (e.g., 'add an umbrella to her hand')
Preserve source identity while making targeted changes
Brand asset text or color swaps with preservation guidance
Reproducible edits with seed parameter for variant comparisons
Aspect ratio selection for output framing
Integration with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for AI workflow
limitations: Accepts only one source image per request (multi-image flows require Nano Banana Edit)
Compound prompts may cause output drift; split into sequential single-instruction passes
Multilingual or embedded text editing often performs better on GPT Image 2
Requires RunComfy account and CLI authentication

use_cases.title: Product Photography Updates
use_cases.description: Update product images with new branding, seasonal changes, or text modifications while preserving the original composition and lighting.

use_cases.title: Portrait Retouching
use_cases.description: Make targeted adjustments to portrait photos such as adding accessories, changing clothing items, or adjusting expressions while maintaining facial identity.

use_cases.title: Marketing Asset Iteration
use_cases.description: Quickly generate variants of marketing visuals for A/B testing by making small compositional changes while maintaining brand consistency.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Object Addition
prompt_templates.prompt: Keep the person's face, pose, and clothing unchanged. Add [object description] in [location].
prompt_templates.scenario: When you want to add a specific item to an image without changing other elements

prompt_templates.title: Brand Asset Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Keep the [product] silhouette, table surface, and lighting exactly as in the input. Replace only [specific element], from '[old]' to '[new]'.
prompt_templates.scenario: When updating brand assets, labels, or text in product images

prompt_templates.title: Color or Style Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Keep [subject] unchanged. Change [specific aspect] to [new description].
prompt_templates.scenario: When making color adjustments or style changes to specific elements

prompt_templates.title: Iterative Refinement
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the previous edit. Keep all elements as they are except [new change].
prompt_templates.scenario: When building complex edits through multiple sequential passes

output_examples.input: Keep the person's face, pose, and clothing unchanged. Add a leather shoulder bag, dark brown, hanging on the right shoulder.
output_examples.output: The model outputs an edited image with the bag added while maintaining all other elements of the original portrait.

output_examples.input: Keep the bottle silhouette, table, and lighting exactly as in the input. Replace only the brand text on the label, from 'ALPHA' to 'AURA'. Same font weight, white on black, centered.
output_examples.output: The model generates a product image with the brand text updated while preserving the original composition and visual quality.

best_practices: Lead with preservation instructions: 'Keep [element] unchanged' before specifying the change
Use single declarative instructions describing one specific change per request
Iterate with small changes rather than compound edits for better fidelity
anti_patterns: Compound prompts with multiple changes ('change A and add B and remove C') cause drift
Passing multiple source images when a single primary reference is needed
Passive voice prompts reduce reliability; use imperative mood for instructions

faq.question: How is Flux Kontext different from Flux 2 Klein?
faq.answer: Flux Kontext is for editing existing images with a reference. Flux 2 Klein is for generating images from scratch with no source image.

faq.question: Can I edit multiple images at once?
faq.answer: No, Flux Kontext accepts only one source image. For batch edits across 1-20 images, use Nano Banana Edit instead.

faq.question: How do I get consistent results for comparison?
faq.answer: Use the seed parameter to reproduce the same output. This is useful for generating variants and comparing different prompt approaches.

faq.question: Why did my compound prompt produce unexpected results?
faq.answer: Flux Kontext drifts with compound prompts. Split complex edits into sequential single-instruction passes for better fidelity.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how much can change in one edit?
faq.answer: Large changes may cause unintended modifications. Start with small targeted edits and iterate. Major style changes are better done through Flux 2 Klein.

faq.question: How does the skill handle authentication?
faq.answer: Use 'runcomfy login' for interactive authentication or set RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable for CI/CD environments.
